validation_comments: Agree with not performing any group comparisons-as it says in the supplemental material:" Numbers in brackets represent different training groups (e.g., [1] = Group 1, [2] = Group 2, etc.)". Also agree with hedges g (with values from the MA). In my calculations CI was close, but not exact (rounding error?).

Calculation method: 
SMD of change scores by change score SDs (Franchi et al stated SEM - these values weren't adjusted in MA)
- with these values and the variance estimator set to Borenstein, I was able to recalculate the CIs

MA references to their preregistration, in which they stated to use post-scores to calculate SMD with 95% CI. „For intervention studies, the postintervention data will be used to calculate the between-group difference while controlling for baseline differences.“

I could not recalculate the ES for Hammond too. Not sure, if it's because of the data extraction. I tried the second study (Hearris et al) and there I was able to recalculate the effect size (nearly) with change scores and an imputation of r = 0.5.
Formula of to calculate SDchange is mentioned, but there was no comment on imputation of r.
